creator: wietse@porcupine.org
text:
Below is a debugger transcript after compiling my 7.1/i386 kernel
with ddb support. This shows that at the time of the panic, the
kernel stack looks very much what is discussed in:

http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-current/2009-July/008901.html

Unfortunately the patch referenced in that article does not apply
to FreeBSD 7.1 (or 7.2). I'll look at FreeBSD 8.0 in a couple days.

	Wietse

tail# kldload vboxdrv
[thread pid 11 tid 100002 ]
Stopped at      0:      *** error reading from address 0 ***
db> bt
Tracing pid 11 tid 100002 td 0xc6345af0
rtmpOnSpecificFreeBSDWrapper([omitted]) at rtmpOnSpecificFreeBSDWrapper+0x3d
smp_rendezvous_action([omitted] at smp_rendezvous_action+0x9b
Xrendezvous() at Xrendezvous+0x1e
--- interrupt, eip = 0xc0e70615, esp = 0xe4f0ac88, ebp = 0xe4f0ac88 ---
acpi_cpu_c1(e4f0acc0,c07a4fb6,c6345af0,3,1,...) at acpi_cpu_c1+0x5
acpi_cpu_idle(e4f0acf4,c07bcbc4,1,0,c0c64000,...) at acpi_cpu_idle+0x184
cpu_idle(1,0,c0c64000,c6345af0,202,...) at cpu_idle+0x28
sched_idletd(0,e4f0ad38,0,0,0,...) at sched_idletd+0x2c4
fork_exit(c07bc900,0,e4f0ad38) at fork_exit+0x99
fork_trampoline() at fork_trampoline+0x8
--- trap 0, eip = 0, esp = 0xe4f0ad70, ebp = 0 ---

With kernel symbols translated after-the-fact, this is my reconstruction
of the machine stack:

acpi_cpu_c1()
    acpica/acpi_machdep.c:551   __asm __volatile("sti; hlt");
cpu_idle()
    i386/machdep.c:1183         (*cpu_idle_hook)()
sched_idletd(arg = 0, frame = e4f0ad38)
    kern/sched_ule.c:2676       cpu_idle();
fork_exit(callout = sched_idletd, arg = 0, frame = e4f0ad38)
    kern/kern_fork.c:804        callout(arg = 0, frame = e4f0ad38)
fork_trampoline()
    i386/exception.s:260     ENTRY(fork_trampoline)
    i386/exception.s:261             pushl   %esp      /* trapframe pointer */
    i386/exception.s:262             pushl   %ebx      /* arg1 */
    i386/exception.s:263             pushl   %esi      /* function */
    i386/exception.s:264             call    fork_exit
